branch_metadata.branch_rationale: Einstein-equation source residual / GR emergence diagnostic; T_00, G_00, Lambda_t are matter-side / source-side per P4 manuscript

title: Nine-point curvature-side and topological-witness ladders for Einstein-gap exponent alpha = 2/3

primary_curvature_side_witness.source: h3c_vollausbau lattice run; R_bar aggregate from per-regime A2-npz

reviewer_hedging.interpretation: Both ladders are indirect witnesses tied to Delta_E by the curvature/chirality bridge; they are not pointwise tensor-residual identifications.
reviewer_hedging.free_fit_caveat: Power-law fits without a fixed alpha do not uniquely select alpha=2/3; the value alpha=2/3 is fixed analytically by Theorem 15.18 P2.
reviewer_hedging.skip_N18_caveat: N=18 is a finite-size boundary regime; skip-N18 variants improve R^2 but introduce a finite-size selection.
reviewer_hedging.chirality_bridge_status: Empirically established: Pearson r(chirality_dev, R_bar) = 0.89, r^2 = 0.80 across all 9 regimes; robust to skip-N=18 (r=0.89, r^2=0.79). Linear regression: chirality_dev = 0.31 * R_bar + 0.02. The Atiyah-Singer-type formal index theorem on emergent geometry that would turn this empirical correlation into an analytic identity is future work.

chirality_curvature_correlation.interpretation: Chirality-deviation tracks ~31% of the Ricci-scalar amplitude across the 9-point ladder, with a small positive intercept ~0.02 consistent with a distinct topological floor; the correlation r=0.89 is the empirical content of the chirality-curvature bridge.
